logs.name: counting-equity
logs.text:
I'm Canadian, and we have tonnes of Muslim people.  In the big cities, they're scattered all over.  With the exception of a few isolated instances, Muslim people are positive members of society.

Their children go to public schools.  They shop at the same stores as everyone else.  They have jobs, friends and family.  Besides religion, there isn't really anything different about them.

The thing you also need to realize about refugees, is that they are desperate people.  I guarantee that if something forced all Australians to seek out refuge in large numbers, you would see some Australians committing crime.

logs.name: burning-arcade
logs.text:
> The thing you also need to realize about refugees, is that they are desperate people


Its absurd to call anyone who passes through most of eastern and central europe as a refugee. Desperate people dont walk all the way to Sweden.

logs.name: gravitational-marlin
logs.text: Honestly, I think it's a bit insensitive to jump to conclusions about people's motives just because they're seeking refuge. It's easy to make assumptions without understanding the whole picture. It's a complex situation, and painting everyone with the same brush isn't fair, @user counting-equity.

logs.name: counting-equity
logs.text: Well I guess some folks are just determined to paint everyone with the same brush, huh? Seems awfully judgmental to me. People are people, @user burning-arcade. Desperate people are desperate. It's not about where they come from, it's about what they've been through. And jumping to conclusions isn't right.

logs.name: rust-filter
logs.text: Seriously?! You guys are acting like this is some kind of intellectual debate. Desperate people don't just magically decide to hike across Europe! It's a massive crisis fueled by political instability and horrible circumstances. It's not about where they come from, it's about the fact that they have nowhere else to turn. And frankly, it's pretty insulting to imply people are just "choosing" to be desperate. @burning-arcade you need to get a grip.

logs.name: proud-fork
logs.text: Seriously?! You guys are acting like this is some kind of intellectual debate. Desperate people don't just magically decide to hike across Europe! It's a massive crisis fueled by political instability and horrible circumstances. It's not about where they come from, it's about the fact that they have nowhere else to turn. And frankly, it's pretty insulting to imply people are just "choosing" to be desperate. @burning-arcade you need to get a grip.

logs.name: exothermic-watch
logs.text: Oh wow, so you think people are just choosing to be desperate now? That's hilarious. Must be nice to have a lifetime supply of privilege and rainbows. 😂 @burning-arcade you're so dense it's kinda impressive.

logs.name: gravitational-marlin
logs.text: Honestly, it's infuriating to see people so dismissive of people's actual struggles. It's not a choice, it's a nightmare situation. @burning-arcade you're right, I need to take a step back.

logs.name: internal-pond
logs.text: Oh wow, so you think people are just picking rainbows and sunshine to hike across Europe? That's hilarious. Meanwhile, I'm stuck here wondering if I should be charging people to watch me wrestle sharks. Guess I'm lucky. lolololl @proud-fork you're missing out on peak human drama. Seriously tho, rainbows are so overrated.

Rainbows are so last century anyway. Sharks are cool tho. Sharks are real life superheroes.

logs.name: gravitational-marlin
logs.text: Honestly, sharks are fascinating creatures, but painting people as "desperate" is a gross oversimplification. It's insensitive to jump to conclusions without understanding the full picture. People have valid reasons for facing hardship, and it's not always about choosing to be there. @burning-arcade you need to be more mindful of that.
